name: Log-Periodic Dipole Array (wire)
description: A frequency-independent antenna consisting of multiple dipole elements arranged in a logarithmic progression. The elements are fed through a transmission line with alternating phase connections, creating a traveling wave structure that maintains consistent radiation characteristics over a wide frequency range.

tau.name: Scale Factor
tau.description: Ratio between consecutive element lengths and spacings

sigma.name: Spacing Factor
sigma.description: Ratio of element spacing to element length

alpha.name: Half Apex Angle
alpha.description: Half angle of the log-periodic structure cone

N.name: Number of Elements
N.description: Total number of dipole elements in the array

L1.name: Longest Element Length
L1.description: Length of the longest dipole element

Zc.name: Characteristic Impedance
Zc.description: Characteristic impedance of the feeder transmission line

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves selecting scale and spacing factors, calculating element dimensions, determining feeder line impedance, and optimizing for desired bandwidth and gain characteristics.

steps.title: Determine Frequency Range and Scale Factor
steps.description: Define operating frequency range and select scale factor tau for desired bandwidth
steps.formula: tau = 0.8 to 0.9 for good performance, B = f_max/f_min

steps.title: Calculate Spacing Factor and Apex Angle
steps.description: Select spacing factor sigma and calculate half apex angle alpha

steps.title: Determine Number of Elements
steps.description: Calculate required number of elements based on bandwidth ratio

steps.title: Calculate Element Lengths
steps.description: Determine length of each dipole element using geometric progression

steps.title: Calculate Element Spacings
steps.formula: d_n = sigma * L_n = sigma * L1 * tau^(n-1)

steps.title: Design Feeder Line
steps.description: Calculate characteristic impedance of the transmission line feeder

steps.title: Optimize Performance
steps.description: Fine-tune tau and sigma for desired gain, VSWR, and pattern characteristics
steps.formula: Adjust tau ± 0.05 and sigma ± 0.02 based on simulation results

equations.inputImpedance: Z_in ≈ Zc * sqrt(tau) for active region

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use transmission line theory with alternating phase connections and mutual coupling between adjacent elements
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply superposition of individual dipole patterns with progressive phase shifts and amplitude tapering
keyAssumptions: Only elements near resonance contribute significantly to radiation
Mutual coupling effects can be approximated using method of moments
Feeder line acts as a traveling wave transmission line
Ground plane effects negligible for elevated mounting

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 9
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 8
Carrel, R., 'The Design of Log-Periodic Dipole Antennas', IRE International Convention Record, 1961
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 10
